0.q: Silencing of CsATG6 decreased autophagic activity and salt tolerance, whereas its overexpression enhanced both.
Overexpression of CsATG6 in Arabidopsis enhanced seedling root growth under nitrogen deficiency, drought, and salt stress, and improved survival rates following high-salinity treatment.

1.q: Pn and Tr of CsPAO2-silencing plants were significantly less than those of pV190-silencing plants and suffered more from limitation of salt stress on photosynthesis.

4.q: the transcript levels of photosynthesis-related genes were higher in CsTGase OE plants than the WT plants under normal conditions

5.q: overexpression of CsSHMT3 gene in cucumber seedlings resulted in a significant increase in chlorophyll content, photosynthetic rate and proline (Pro) content, and antioxidant enzyme activity under salt stress condition

6.q: the activities of SOD, POD, CAT and APX of CsPP2-A1-OE cucumber increased rapidly and maintained at a higher level; CsPP2-A1 brought a salinity tolerance ability to cucumber through ... reactive oxygen species (ROS) homeostasis.

7.q: CsBPC2 modulates the scavenging of ROS in response to salt stress by regulating SOD, POD, CAT, APX, and DHAR activities.

10.q: Knockout mutants of CsSGR generated by CRISPR/Cas9 exhibit enhanced LT tolerance, in particularly, increased chlorophyll (Chl) content and reduced reactive oxygen species (ROS) accumulation in response to LT.

11.q: CsROS1b-mediated CG hypomethylation of CsATG6 enhances salt stress tolerance by activating autophagy in cucumber.

12.q: low temperature and high salinity also elevated free IAA level in cucumber, which may mainly due to the dramatically increased CsYUC10b expression.

13.q: as the expression level of CsYUC11 increase, the pedicel length as well as the IAA content increase progressively, implying that CsYUC11 promotes pedicel elongation through auxin biosynthesis in Arabidopsis.

14.q: the four parameters of CsPSA3-silencing plants were all obviously higher than the other two silencing plants, indicating that downregulation of CsPSA3 was beneficial to cucumber photosynthesis and growth under salt stress.

15.q: CsPPH knockout prevents saline–alkali stress-induced chlorophyll degradation and alleviates the inhibition of photosynthesis; CsPPH functions as a negative regulator of both photosynthesis and saline–alkali tolerance in cucumber.

16.q: Co-IP assay showed that CsPAO2 interacted with PSA3 in plants. BiFC assay confirmed this positive interaction, YFP fluorescent signals were observed in tobacco leaves co-infected with CsPAO2-YFPn and PSA3-YFPc.

17.q: Through screening the yeast two-hybrid library and combined with yeast two-hybrid and GST pull-down, the interaction of CsGPA1 with CsTIP1.1 was found the first time in a plant.

18.q: Immunoblot (IB) analyses with an anti-HA antibody revealed the interaction between CsCDPK6-Flag and CsSAMS1-HA in vivo. Y2H and BiFC confirmed interaction between CsSAMS1 and CsCDPK6.

19.q: the silencing of ATG4 and ATG7 reduced Spd-induced salt tolerance and autophagosomes formation.

20.q: the silencing of ATG4 and ATG7 reduced Spd-induced salt tolerance and autophagosomes formation.

22.q: ATG6 (Beclin1 in mammals) is a core component of the phosphatidylinositol 3-kinase (PI3K) complex, essential for autophagosome initiation.

24.q: the expression of CsYUC10b was induced 20 fold while CsYUC4 was decreased 5.5 fold under low temperature.

25.q: suppression of CsGPA1 resulted in increased expression of CsTIP1.1 in the leaves and roots, suggesting that CsGPA1 may act as a suppressor of CsTIP1.1

26.q: After salt stress treatment, the SAM content in CsCDPK6-silenced lines was significantly higher than that in WT plants, indicating that CsCDPK6 negatively regulated endogenous SAM synthesis.

27.q: the expression of stress-related genes SOD, CAT, SOS1, SOS2, NHX, and HKT was significantly up-regulated by overexpressing CsSHMT3 gene in cucumber seedlings; while stress-related gene expression showed significant decrease in silenced CsSHMT3 gene seedlings

28.q: the expression of stress-related genes SOD, CAT, SOS1, SOS2, NHX, and HKT was significantly up-regulated by overexpressing CsSHMT3 gene in cucumber seedlings; while stress-related gene expression showed significant decrease in silenced CsSHMT3 gene seedlings

33.q: The expression patterns of LOC101219529 and LOC105434443 were the same as CsTAU1, and the introduction of CsTAU1 (+) increased the chlorophyll, α-Naphthylamine oxidation, glutathione S-transferase (GST), and catalase (CAT) content of cucumber.

34.q: The expression patterns of LOC101219529 and LOC105434443 were the same as CsTAU1, and the introduction of CsTAU1 (+) increased the chlorophyll, α-Naphthylamine oxidation, glutathione S-transferase (GST), and catalase (CAT) content of cucumber.

35.q: CsROS1b expression, which actively demethylated CsATG6 at CG sites and increased CsATG6 transcript abundance, enhancing autophagosome formation

36.q: Electrophoretic mobility shift assays (EMSA) confirmed the binding of CsCBF1 to the CsSGR promoter in vitro... these results suggest that CsCBF1 is able to activate the expression of CsSGR by directly binding to its promoter.

37.q: Y1H and electrophoretic mobility shift assay (EMSA) results demonstrated that CsERF113L bound to both "GCCGAC" elements in the CsPPH promoter... Dual-LUC assays confirmed that CsERF113L activated CsPPH transcription.

38.q: CsERF113L promotes chlorophyll degradation... through direct transcriptional upregulation of CsPPH, chlorophyll b reductase (CsNYC1), and chlorophyllase 2 (CsCLH2).

39.q: CsERF113L promotes chlorophyll degradation... through direct transcriptional upregulation of CsPPH, chlorophyll b reductase (CsNYC1), and chlorophyllase 2 (CsCLH2).

40.q: CsERF113L positively regulated ETH biosynthesis under saline–alkali stress by upregulating the transcription of 1-aminocyclopropane-1-carboxylic acid synthase 6/9/10 (CsACS6/9/10).

41.q: indirectly stimulating ethylene synthesis via upregulation of 1-aminocyclopropane-1-carboxylic acid synthase 6/9/10 (CsACS6/9/10).

42.q: indirectly stimulating ethylene synthesis via upregulation of 1-aminocyclopropane-1-carboxylic acid synthase 6/9/10 (CsACS6/9/10).

43.q: CsRAP2.12 activates the transcription of both ethylene response factor 113-like (CsERF113L) and CsRAP2.7.

44.q: CsRAP2.12 activates the transcription of both ethylene response factor 113-like (CsERF113L) and CsRAP2.7.

45.q: CsERF113L further transcriptionally regulates CsRAP2.7... Additionally, CsERF113L functioned upstream of CsRAP2.7 to exert transcriptional regulation.

46.q: This enhancement was accompanied by elevated relative expression of respiratory burst oxidase homologue (RBOH) genes RbohD and RbohF and a higher NADPH oxidase activity.

47.q: This enhancement was accompanied by elevated relative expression of respiratory burst oxidase homologue (RBOH) genes RbohD and RbohF and a higher NADPH oxidase activity.

48.q: RBOH-dependent H2O2 mediated the Spd-induced autophagy and salt tolerance in cucumber.

49.q: The increase of galactinol content in plant leaves, both endogenously through the transgene expression and exogenously through the sugar treatment, conferred disease resistance against bacterial and fungal infections.

50.q: Under LT stress, CsSGR interacts with CsNYC1 to mediate Chl degradation. Knockout mutants of CsSGR exhibit increased chlorophyll content.

51.q: CsERF113L promotes chlorophyll degradation and reactive oxygen species (ROS) accumulation both through direct transcriptional upregulation of CsPPH, CsNYC1, and CsCLH2.

52.q: CsERF113L promotes chlorophyll degradation and reactive oxygen species (ROS) accumulation both through direct transcriptional upregulation of CsPPH, CsNYC1, and CsCLH2.

53.q: CsERF113L promotes chlorophyll degradation and reactive oxygen species (ROS) accumulation both through direct transcriptional upregulation of CsPPH, CsNYC1, and CsCLH2.

54.q: repression of CsCDPK6 expression by virus-induced gene silencing (VIGS) in cucumber seedling cotyledons under salt stress increased ethylene production

55.q: The LT-sensitive haplotype CsSGRHapA, but not the LT-tolerant haplotype CsSGRHapG could interact with NON-YELLOW COLORING 1 (CsNYC1) to mediate Chl degradation.

56.q: the LT‐sensitive haplotype CsSGRHapA, but not the LT‐tolerant haplotype CsSGRHapG could interact with NON‐YELLOW COLORING 1 (CsNYC1) to mediate Chl degradation.

57.q: CsTAU1 was screened from the glutathione pathway from previous transcriptome data for cloning and functional analysis.

58.q: STAYGREEN (SGR) encodes magnesium dechelatase, a key regulator of the chlorophyll (Chl) degradation pathway.

59.q: Spd induced the expression of RBOH (respiratory burst oxidase homolog), and the accumulation of H2O2 both in leaves and roots.

60.q: the activity of H2S synthesis-related enzymes LCD, DCD, and CAS, and the expression of H2S synthesis-related genes (CsLCD and CsDCD)

61.q: the activity of H2S synthesis-related enzymes LCD, DCD, and CAS, and the expression of H2S synthesis-related genes (CsLCD and CsDCD)

62.q: CsPAO2 overexpression could promote Put's conversion to Spd and Spm to keep the (Spd+Spm)/Put ratio from decreasing.

63.q: This enhancement was accompanied by elevated relative expression of respiratory burst oxidase homologue (RBOH) genes RbohD and RbohF and a higher NADPH oxidase activity.

64.q: This enhancement was accompanied by elevated relative expression of respiratory burst oxidase homologue (RBOH) genes RbohD and RbohF and a higher NADPH oxidase activity.

65.q: The decreased leaf Na+ content of pumpkin-grafted plants was achieved by higher Na+ exclusion in roots, which was driven by the Na+/H+ antiporter energized by the plasma membrane H+-ATPase, as evidenced by the higher plasma membrane H+-ATPase activity and higher transcript levels for PMA and SOS1.

66.q: The galactose donor, galactinol, is produced from UDP-Gal and myo-inositol via galactinol synthase (GolS) during the initial step in the biosynthesis of RFO.

67.q: Overexpression of CsTLP8 also increased the sensitivity to ABA during seed germination and ABA-mediated stomatal closure.

68.q: RNA sequencing identified CsPPH, a pheophytinase-encoding gene that functions as a negative regulator of both photosynthesis and saline-alkali tolerance in cucumber.
